compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: LIVE-CHECK ATTESTATION (dogfood, 2026-07-13): the deployed auto-lander performed a live rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader + submit end-to-end landing change #618 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/618) on main. Evidence: main advanced under #618 after Autosubmit was set, so the bot REBASED it to a fresh Patch Set 3 via POST /changes/{id}/rebase with rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true (submitter recorded as 'Rebar Bot Nava'; the original uploader + DCO Signed-off-by + rebar-ticket trailers preserved on PS3, which then earned a fresh Verified+1 and merged). Single-change routing (POST /rebase) exercised live; the rebase:chain multi-member variant is the same on_behalf mechanism exercised by the unit suite (test_multi_member_chain_uses_rebase_chain + on_behalf body assertion). Selection FIFO + classify + routing unit-verified (tests/autolander/test_s2a_*).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The serial auto-lander's first step: pick the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain and route it to the correct Gerrit rebase call. Split out of S2 (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`) to keep each unit loadable whole.

## Files
- `infra/autolander/loop.py` — selection + rebase-routing (part of the core loop).
- `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` — thin STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit helper (the `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` `urllib`+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency, no import of `src/rebar`), covering the calls this loop needs (get change, RelatedChanges, rebase, rebase:chain, submit, DETAILED_LABELS).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Selection:** selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain; **FIFO keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's `ApprovalInfo.date`** (read via `GET /changes/?q=…&o=DETAILED_LABELS`), oldest-voted-submittable-first.
- [ ] **Rebase-routing:** detect a relation chain via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related`; a >1-member non-merge chain rebases with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` (confirmed on this instance's docs; linear ancestry only, no `committer_email`); a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (first-parent-only `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`), NEVER `rebase:chain`; a single non-merge change uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`.
- [ ] `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves the uploader + DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer (keeps the human as uploader; the rebase drops `Verified` so CI re-runs).
- [ ] **Gerrit helper** reuses this repo's STDLIB-ONLY pattern (not `pygerrit2`, not a `src/rebar` import); the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5).
- [ ] **Tests:** unit-test the FIFO ordering + the merge-vs-chain-vs-single routing against recorded Gerrit JSON; a live check exercises `rebase:chain` + `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance **and asserts, on the rebased patchset, that the uploader is unchanged (still the original human) and the `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailers are preserved** (the reason for `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`).
compiled_state.title: S2a Selection + rebase-routing (FIFO, RelatedChanges, rebase/rebase:chain) + stdlib Gerrit helper
